title: guide-for-beginners
content:
Guide for beginers

Screen layout

The screen is divided into 5 sections. Each of these sections serves to the specific purpose.

Pic. 1. Screen layout

Individual sections of the Bedobe

  1. Toolbar

  2. Scene outliner

  3. Mode and selection options

  4. Axis orientation and SW info

  5. 3d Viewport

Toolbar

A set of tools for controlling and configuring the Bedobe app is available.

Pic. 2. Bodobe toolbar

Scene outliner

There is a set of tool for exploring the model hierarchie. If the scene outliner is opend and the user select any object in the viewport the particular object will be selected in the scene outliner as well, and vice versa if user select any object in the scene outliner, then the object is highlighted in the 3D viewport. There is a context menu assigneg to the each item of the three.

Next there is search input - if user write any string, then only object coresponding to the string occure. There is replate button next to the search input. This button opens advanced dialog which can be used for bulk name replacement. Regular expression can be used in the replace dialog.

Pic. 3. Scene outliner

Mode options

This is probably the post important section for current users, who does not use advanced features. In this list, the user selects the mode for working with the software they wish to use.

Currently, the following modes are available:

  • Navigate - The basic mode is used to select and move individual objects or assemblies.

  • Add Measure - Add distance measurements for an object

  • Add Angle - Add angle measurements for an object

  • Add Dim (Flat) - adds dimensions to an object that remain the same size regardless of the zoom level.

  • Add Dim (3D) - adds dimensions to an object. These dimensions scale up and down as you zoom in or out on the object.

  • Add Annotation (Flat) - adds annotations to an object that remain the same size regardless of the object's zoom level.

  • Add Annotation (3D) - přidávání poznámek objektu. Tyto poznámky se zvětšují a zmenšují se zoom objektem.

  • Edit labes - režim pro úpravu poznámek a kót

  • Assembly Edit - tento režim slouží k vytváření pracovních postupů montáže a demontáže sestav. Může být zvolen pouze ze toolbaru "assembly".

Selection options

Pic. 4. Mode options

In this list, the user can select the type of selection depth.

  • CAD selection - the user selects entire objects.

  • Detailed selection - corresponds to "mesh selection" in Three.js. If an object's faces are displayed in different ways, each method will correspond to a single detailed selection.

  • No selection - suitable for demonstrations where you do not want any objects to be selected.

Pic. 5. Selection options

Axis orientation and SW info

In this section, the user can see the current orientation of the coordinate system. The gizmo is also interactive and is used for precise alignment with the planes. Basic information is then displayed at the bottom

3d Viewport

This section provides a 3D view of the model. When in "Navigate" mode, the user can select and manipulate individual parts of the object. Manipulation is possible for both individual parts and subassemblies. Within the hierarchy, you can navigate using the left and right arrow keys.
